name: Voter Action Project
subject: 185 days to go
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
There are 185 days until the midterm general elections. Primaries have already started. State legislative sessions are in full swing, with more right around the corner, and suppressive bills are on the rise again.
What does this all mean? Our work is going full speed right now , not waiting for the summer. The foundation we lay right now is what sets the stage for November's elections.
RUSH $35 or more right now and help make every vote count and every voice heard in our elections nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
The Atlantic: Donald Trump’s Plan to Subvert the Midterms Is Already Under Way [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=25]
Team, that headline from The Atlantic says it all.
Donald Trump already promised as part of his 2024 campaign that “ we’ll have it fixed so good, you’re not gonna have to vote .” Watching his actions after he lost in 2020 -- lies, denial, rallies, all leading to January 6th -- it's not hard to imagine what he's got in mind for November.
Our states run our elections. But that doesn't mean that MAGA-aligned politicians won't follow his illegal orders to interfere. It doesn't stop him from using ICE agents to intimidate at the polls or threaten eligible voters. And it doesn't stop his Department of Justice from trying to interfere through the courts, especially when more than half of the suppressive lawsuits brought to court this year were brought by the very federal department that's supposed to protect our rights.
None of that takes into account the highly partisan efforts for mid-decade redistricting as well. It's clear that the threats to the next election aren't going to stop. That's where we come in.
We must inform voters, defend our elections, and make sure every eligible vote counts. Chip in with $35 or whatever you can today to fuel our voting rights work for 2026 and beyond >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
Our democracy, our elections, and our fundamental right to vote have been under attack for more than a decade. Our electoral system is reaching a breaking point, and it's hard to predict exactly where the next set of electoral shenanigans will come from.
Are we destined to watch our elections break down? Absolutely not. We still have the time and the people to win. What we need most is the resources to carry on this fight for our democracy and the work to make every voice heard in our elections.
Stopping their attempts to subvert the midterms will require courage and integrity across the board. Most of all, it will depend on individual Americans like us to stand up for our rights and demand that our votes are counted.
This is a fight we can and will win. Stand with us to protect the right to vote by pitching in with $35 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050226_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050226_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. Hello,
It’s Eileen Laubacher, the retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral and Democrat running against Lauren Boebert here in Colorado. And I am emailing to humbly ask if you can make a donation to my campaign for Congress today to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue.
Lauren Boebert barely won reelection against Adam Frisch. He lost by just 546 votes in what was the closest race in the entire country.
Now, after retiring as a two-star Admiral, I’m running to defeat Lauren Boebert because I know we can send her packing and turn this seat blue – but I’m counting on grassroots donors like you to give now if we’re going to do it.
[**Please, will you make a monthly donation to my campaign now to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats? When we finally kick Lauren Boebert out of Congress once and for all, you’ll be able to say you were a critical part of it from the start.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130637673/618413104/156431970?refcode=20260502pm-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMGMxY2JhMGItODM0Ni1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=kg_V5grLmkAAf20woUmn9P4FF2nG4_lhzuCWIGBK0_s=)

Now, after retiring as a two-star Admiral, I’m running to defeat Lauren Boebert because I know we can send her packing and turn this seat blue – but I’m counting on grassroots donors like you to give now if we’re going to do it. = Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = I’m not taking money from corporate PACs like Boebert is, so I have nowhere else to turn. I am incredibly grateful for any amount you can chip in today. Thank you, Eileen Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher (Retired) United States Navy ** Paid for by Eileen for Colorado Eileen for Colorado 9249 S Broadway #200-172 Highlands Ranch, CO 80129 United States Contributions or gifts to Eileen for Colorado are not tax deductible.

clean_body: | Peter, Before we finalize our Senate strategy, I need to hear from you. | We're at a critical moment. The decisions we make in the coming months will determine what Republicans can deliver for the American people. That's why I put together a strategy poll for our most committed supporters. I want real input from the people who are actually in this fight with us. Thousands have already responded. The results are shaping how we move forward. But I haven't seen your answers yet and that matters to me. | Take a few minutes and tell me what you think. Where should Senate Republicans focus? What issues matter most to you? This is your seat at the table. Sincerely,John Thune | | | Paid for by Thune Victory Committee 228 S. WASHINGTON STREET SUITE 115 ALEXANDRIA, VA 22314 |

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em03132026 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em03132026]
The Supreme Court just handed Republicans a roadmap for wiping out Democratic representation across the South.
They can call special sessions, redraw majority-Black districts, crack Democratic voters into pieces, and try to turn the Voting Rights Act into a dead letter.
Mississippi already moved, and other Republican legislatures are making plans.
But here’s what Republicans know and hope we don’t: when they crack Democratic districts, those voters don’t vanish. They land in nearby Republican seats, turning “safe” GOP districts into real fights.
Their plan can backfire badly, especially in a midterm where Trump is unpopular, independent voters are moving away from MAGA, and Democrats are overperforming in special elections across the country.
But only if we’re ready.
Chip in $10, $25, $50, or whatever you can to help GOTV PAC fight back before these maps become the battlefield: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em05022026]

Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

body:

---
Friend—I just got off the debate stage! While my MAGA-funded Democratic opponent spent his time on stage attacking me with lies and misinformation, I focused on how I am going to deliver for New Mexicans. **I'm in this fight to make life easier for New Mexican families and the future brighter for our kids.**
| | [RUSH $5](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/8176?refcode=jtk654-fr-nd&t=2&akid=9352%2E400544%2EbbAN_K)
---
Tonight, on the debate stage, I shared with voters my bold vision for New Mexico and why my personal and leadership experience in Congress and as Secretary of the Interior makes me the best person to lead our state. My campaign is about lowering costs, making rent and housing more affordable, strengthening our schools, and addressing the root causes of crime so we can make our communities safer. _It’s also about representing New Mexicans, while my opponent is propped up by the same donors that elected Trump._
While there are less than 30 days till the primary, this race doesn’t stop there. There are also three Republicans ready to flip New Mexico. I can't deliver on my vision without grassroots supporters like you.
**_[So if you're feeling as energized as I am in this moment, please chip in $5 now to help me reach my $15,000 goal before midnight tonight:](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/8176?refcode=jtk654-fr-nd&t=3&akid=9352%2E400544%2EbbAN_K)_**

**[Can I count on you to pitch in $5 or whatever makes sense to you to sustain our fight and keep California blue?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260502_3_ak.odggEy&refcode=em20260502-41250&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=41250%2E3422442%2Eo3gWrV)**

Katie Porter

subject: Alice was taken, but she was found
body:
https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUmiDLlG46EhhfKVEOxuwNQe_COV4ry7FJ7r3NZXMb7HT2pU2ZkldTbUnv8hsT2SPyI9bmdCzn4jCiSv7rCZTmkmYA7WKg041RiuA
View in browser
A Special Message from our SponsorPlease note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of Parler and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by Parler.
Sound of FreedomShe was rescued. Many are still waiting. You can help find them. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUnwP3RspeiVGF2Od1sVoCksZL7n2VNDahcCIWj3xlgNynvR97ks38Wrb0dSTy6c7Y4OK4RHuorneLamDSPyu6oRRMit3xA5-ruDA

GOD'S CHILDREN ARE NOT FOR SALE

Friend,

A little girl sat alone in the dark.

Lured online by someone pretending to care and taken from her home.

Kidnapped and hidden away in a remote cabin, where she was abused, and sold online.

And passed from one man to another.

Her name is Alice.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUoSLjqmOdoVycg7NdlyuM3PoNLHiFWU0xTNcSaHzKQDLumt8bHLz6r3MEgA4yk151uNRLOdbwqEk-OpojJAay6cTGRTMbyWpcnSA
Rescue A Child Now

And this is the part most people never hear.

She was found. An investigator in Florida traced her trafficker’s username. He saw a connection others missed and contacted the FBI.

Agents followed the digital trail, tracing the trafficker’s IP address.

They moved in, raided the location and rescued her.

Alive.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUp7IhyQbbrOvPbwREJOgn_mnhdkWlJ9ymcGG0csx2LZuLISph5grE05qBsBEwpM9eB94BwlYbG3Qq4XOmsAt0RM9ipd5XoIKfKNQ

Her trafficker now faces 20 years to life in prison.

This is what happens when someone goes looking.
This is what happens when the right tools are in place.

But there are still so many children who have not been found yet.

Still waiting.
Still trapped.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUq238nDNui0oHkG2isK4zHcwCoSxCzF2GA-Ad3k_sPGy6_mqFXnID7R0SYICuQ5U5HreBWiNRkmi3WtcdYBb-Nn5QhEUZX5gwpVw
Rescue A Child Now

And that is why the Sound of Freedom Foundation exists.

Friend, if this is your first time hearing about us, the Sound of Freedom Foundation’s mission is clear:

To stop the sale of God’s children.

The foundation extends the reach of the Sound of Freedom film, bringing awareness to the reality of child trafficking and taking action to stop it.

Our vision is simple:

A world where every child is safe and free.

We work with partners to prevent exploitation before it begins, support rescue operations, and equip investigators. Our partners walk with survivors as they heal and rebuild their lives.

Because rescue is only the beginning. And right now, there is an urgent need.

We are launching Operation Search and Rescue, a mission to connect investigators and agents, identify traffickers, and build criminal evidence.

We will support real rescue operations using tools like digital tracking and face recognition.

This is how children are found and how traffickers are stopped.
And today, you can be part of it.

And right now, your donation will be matched, dollar for dollar.

A concerned Child Defense Organization has approveda $10,000 MATCHING GRANT.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUrfXRxeGpqpkD7PuOfcbPPMDzFbfTCmkLAPl8snV2HxzvkfnyonRYKTMHPWbo_lhkjIbe-S_--ntDKmJMpOrCYHHkQ85KOXyqpUA

Every dollar you give will be doubled.

$25 Gift Doubles to $50
$50 Gift Doubles to $100
$100 Gift Doubles to $200
$250 Gift Doubles to $500
$500 Gift Doubles to $1,000

Your gift goes twice as far to help find and rescue children.

This is God multiplying love.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUsPE7O5IOzKwKh23gxJ8rP7_tW2eOaT7HlwoHv74KjNZufDsCreLuTNJCiw14nBR_6wwphR54HYjwtIJ1p_IWUCZ1gC1buIX8JWA

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUuQd10dUxUT74qhjrX5ZGvBW_SN0025v-EsTYpLfzV4UiUKKfXDpn-cHvFbX-eWTvVqt9Tb8IrVtFCobsPq2bO_jmOm3cyKQepwg
Your donation of $25, $50, $100 or whatever you can give today can have twice the impact, and double the chance to bring a child home.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUv6Gwk4mDEOXolCaC-SANmk3_Y2IklsGstWeZt5vxv5fGcLybtmaPkqJo1U4M67bHVk-DCam92n5BnzLdN3vmo73xlJmuzae1Qig
Double My Impact Now

Jesus said, “Whoever welcomes one little child like this in my name welcomes me.” (Matthew 18:5)

Today you can be that welcome.
You can enable the search and help the rescue mission to bring a child home.

Please give as generously as you can while your gift is matched.

And please pray for Alice, and for the children still waiting.
And for the day when every child is safe and free.

Because God’s children are not for sale.

With gratitude and prayer,

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NjV9AAwAAAAAAGli_oUwJuKum5SYR50Se5qfQAJsnt2XOTPK3K9egFMa_G8mlkXO7fooby0qIyzxsFfL5VxwB5Gnx3sXxlMOtt1mIcEQeFmt0XIz1zBqUQ

Eduardo Verástegui

name: James O'Keefe
subject: The White House responded to our hidden-camera election fraud footage.
body:
A man handed a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill on a sidewalk in Los Angeles. On the note was a name, an address, and a date of birth

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z972923ino1kp1v0nkgqigobsd2up8va5bmofq08_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")

---
| Dear Patriot,
A man handed a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill on a sidewalk in Los Angeles. On the note was a name, an address, and a date of birth — **a real voter's identity.** “Sign his name,” the man said. “Just write what I tell you to write.”**[**We filmed the whole thing.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4bf2k91utr1dh34pe3b8r4uonvjpdt26cs6fugg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs)

**[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zusc5npkmvc710cbhq6r0a49qkvcpilq24eqld0o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs)What you're about to see is the most explosive hidden-camera footage my team has ever captured. Not speculation. Not anonymous sources.**Actual election crimes committed on camera — in broad daylight — on the streets of Los Angeles.** Over the course of several days, my undercover journalists documented petition circulators handing post-it notes with real voters' names and addresses to homeless individuals, paying them cash — one to three dollars per signature — and instructing them to forge those voters' signatures on official petitions and voter registration forms.We captured it **twenty-eight times.** That's twenty-eight documented instances in just a few days of filming. Which means across Los Angeles County, this is happening **tens of thousands of times.**[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhav4qlnju45gfu0mjie6bqju2d578c83gdlavhg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")****| [▶ WATCH THE HIDDEN-CAMERA FOOTAGE AND CHIP IN $35](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0jg0pkgcrs6up0r2cqhu3o8dnfpbn63bo6ob94g_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")
---
Let me walk you through what our cameras caught.
A voter named Vicky Walker. A voter named Paulson. A voter named Joseph Albright. Real people. Real identities. In each case, a petition coordinator handed a homeless person a sticky note bearing that voter's information and told them to sign as if they were that person.
One coordinator was caught on camera admitting they get paid seven to ten dollars per signature. They're turning around and paying homeless people a fraction of that — a dollar, sometimes three — to commit federal crimes on their behalf.
Let me be clear about what we're talking about: **it is a federal crime to provide false information on a voter registration form. It is a federal crime to offer payment for voter registration.** What we documented is not a gray area. It is multiple felonies, exposed on hidden camera, happening systematically.
The New York Times told America this doesn't happen. Their Sunday edition dismissed election fraud concerns. **We just proved them wrong twenty-eight times on tape.**
And here's what happened next: we brought our footage to the White House. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called it “astounding.” NBC News covered it. The story is breaking through.
**But investigations like this are not free.**
Every dollar you give goes directly to fieldwork: $35 covers a day of undercover equipment costs. $50 covers the background research we do to identify real voters whose identities are being stolen. $100 funds a full day of field operations. $250 helps cover the legal costs of protecting our journalists when powerful people try to shut us down.
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zbh721mo031o1j4844j4s5qcrfs9qioknlfqpt6o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")****
[▶ CHIP IN $50 TO KEEP OUR CAMERAS ROLLING](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrtrr8ub3bg5asmrut0lonhts6vq5t63h5t48180_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")
---
Think about what's at stake. If petition circulators can forge voter signatures by the tens of thousands in broad daylight — paying homeless people with cash and cigarettes to impersonate real voters — and the system does nothing about it, then your vote is being diluted. Your voice is being stolen.
The only reason we know about this is because my team was there with hidden cameras. **No one else was going to expose this.** Not the Times. Not local officials. Not the people getting paid seven dollars a signature to look the other way.
We did it. And we need your help to keep doing it.
Can I count on you today?
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zt9fuflmrbeik56fubkgq1mujrq734h06s1gbqug_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")****
[▶ YES — RUSH $100 TO FUND THE NEXT INVESTIGATION](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmvrpdnoip3qg7i9654lbshjull3824kisnjb8go_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")
---

In Truth,
James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

**P.S.** They said election fraud doesn't happen. We filmed it twenty-eight times in a matter of days — real voters' identities, forged by strangers for a dollar a signature. The White House has seen it. NBC has covered it. But the investigation isn't over. **Your gift of $35, $50, or $100 today puts our cameras back on the street tomorrow.**
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z42kliaja2ievjq2k7jm7ltfaolp8bsdupc28ebo_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")****
[Chip in right now.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2znsaputdte6daaqqcseeecs2kc3f26nbqkamihdg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-doesnt-happen-mb-popa?utm_source=omg-doesnt-happen&utm_medium=popa&utm_campaign=20260503&utm_content=aan&amount=25")
---

Pop Acta Media | American Action News
---
_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z21snt7fqcb9kkbn0qr3tbfnr3v96nir8hv48kko_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkgqracjkmoq37d1m65qbfctjmkrncst66uqrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb4d9nkvijbo9getqb3cdkmppmeo0n6mq39cdjkorrbcp4mni6ap73cjh6aoh1k8gn48n24hjmepr0s1gm4pb0c8iv0)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6ppffpggdse21nn4766o8oab5oup7jlqujrhu9o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h2bs)_

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zgqudfud40veqt8u8cj5at4sbi9evak3k4fi0c9g_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c3g62oj5c1h238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: J. Davis (BREAKING NEWS)
subject: I’m heartbroken. But I’m not giving up on this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
When they can't win, they bend and break the rules.
| | | | | [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XAeITrI5A_qVgPRiqPWIftND7ckEFYHxQ41MAk2XsNJDGDIQDsYmvLY99-UuYwqhIImNd1j0kGqKdpcr_uk_wERsgT5R2brXwdjG4fihXC84xh6QHaBV-4xGs98ICicdQfLPSDD_KYIMsC_r6pMilVCD9vTP3wfjNblWzjIIHRtAVU7RRZgPW3xaMkFYHuqJoQ3DZyfFYoZ99fUwxxN6JjqsjFu_3ZOI&c=CH1A8wjssKX7s9D4glGnv9KoLDscvuYaWmXIHxo_euKJpFyyBhccyQ==&ch=HanaO_Ds4oqinh6zwdrO6Rk6d_YNpiWoxRMCZz4sjT13tEepdwFlJg==)
---
Peter, I need to be honest with you about how I'm feeling right now.
I'm angry. I'm heartbroken. And I'm more determined than ever.
The Supreme Court just gutted the Voting Rights Act -- a law that civil rights activists fought, bled, and died for. A law that finally gave Black Americans in the South the power to make our voices heard at the ballot box.
And now MAGA extremists have destroyed it.
If that wasn't enough, Speaker Mike Johnson just backed Governor Landry's plan to "postpone" our elections.
---
| [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XAeITrI5A_qVgPRiqPWIftND7ckEFYHxQ41MAk2XsNJDGDIQDsYmvLY99-UuYwqhIImNd1j0kGqKdpcr_uk_wERsgT5R2brXwdjG4fihXC84xh6QHaBV-4xGs98ICicdQfLPSDD_KYIMsC_r6pMilVCD9vTP3wfjNblWzjIIHRtAVU7RRZgPW3xaMkFYHuqJoQ3DZyfFYoZ99fUwxxN6JrgYmrrUzdZm&c=CH1A8wjssKX7s9D4glGnv9KoLDscvuYaWmXIHxo_euKJpFyyBhccyQ==&ch=HanaO_Ds4oqinh6zwdrO6Rk6d_YNpiWoxRMCZz4sjT13tEepdwFlJg==)
---
This isn't about logistics. This isn't about administrative convenience.
Make absolutely no mistake about it: This is about diluting the political power of Black people.
They gutted the Voting Rights Act so they could do exactly this -- rig the system, suppress the vote, and hold onto power by any means necessary.
But one thing about me: I'm not going to sit back and take this idly.
I'm a third-generation farmer. Hard work, tenacity, and toughness are in my blood. I've spent my life fighting for what's right, and I'm not about to stop now.
Yes, it's been a week of devastating news. The Voting Rights Act gutted. Elections postponed. Our democracy under assault.
But we still have the golden ticket to get us out of this: the chance to FLIP this Senate seat.
When we win in November, we don't just elect Louisiana's first Black Senator since Reconstruction. We send a message that voter suppression will not stand. That democracy matters. That Black voices will be heard whether MAGA likes it or not.
But I need your help. We need to show strength right now -- not weakness.
[Will you rush $50, $25, or even $5 before midnight to help us fight back against this assault on our democracy?](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XAeITrI5A_qVgPRiqPWIftND7ckEFYHxQ41MAk2XsNJDGDIQDsYmvLY99-UuYwqh2gUMaV7ZjcVqLBiexnO6Ipvou4rMErIahLSq8IcTkr8C-T_YPVVfFPM2L81OihHvsBVSGqkYcGT2Tw6w6l2hxRatx95DsrAQ9G7GX4IArb-g4ONisarx9UDGSaf77COVZU4JmAe8GhiMIwlGnajLgwvw5hR6g1mQPxzwKJXsj1M=&c=CH1A8wjssKX7s9D4glGnv9KoLDscvuYaWmXIHxo_euKJpFyyBhccyQ==&ch=HanaO_Ds4oqinh6zwdrO6Rk6d_YNpiWoxRMCZz4sjT13tEepdwFlJg==)

# Sunday, May 3, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1986, Willie Shoemaker, 54, guided 17-1 long shot Ferdinand to a Kentucky Derby win, becoming the race's oldest winning jockey and redeeming his infamous 1957 finish-line misjudgment. [See the 1986 Derby recap here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0Etu6yAUANDV4Fmsa8AGDxg86cmNoqbqpGpnFp9LQkoMMjhpdl-1W-gGzuC8H-ejDjEspxd9RVV9mhe8l4i14jrXlLeCXxnXgIvFkoP9jGh0nCnQYQf9DtjsdIiPmTdOeUSKrEHVCSEkUNnz5qykHo2Rmo_WGin5OPLBsq434KzxnfFNUD8a9MA6AMFoywAco94bYQWM3BAO6EJNa3tJYaln9PGRbri2Nl2bqM615kLYP0InQqdH2urWGiR0OqQ75Le-vG6SsKkEwv7vLh9mf5mGZS_zoT49N6ty-R5iDGUQhMPpqkP8deufMm6KfgcAAP__e_VygQ). Tab broke new ground in the soft drink space in the early 1960s, eventually becoming the most widely recognized America diet soda. Eventually it lost ground to a follow-up product in the Coca-Cola line, as shown in today's "[Whatever Happened To...](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwjAUxfGnsTeiy70OhsFDpYpOsFbqYvnjhpg6cZQYUt6-Sl-h6znSb_h_XuzFpZzG29UNbGpX7MjrkrlWnm0t02Phn4nnxGPgZUrhO7N32SLgYQftDshGl_LLKhlNx4xMks1ea30EPLZK9ubkwJOifauOBAoZOXgfo6KTPkQMrUxm06AF2gNowoYAImHXeR00nJQXCjimWubmXtJYe-7yqzx5bkIZZDZ9rdMi6E3gWeB5XdfmVR714Xn7t8XV0As6PwW980fI169-uFOQs4nTmnJOy0ELBbfBpfxH1n91eBr8DQAA__8VlXEx)" In case you missed it, this week on _The Flyover Podcast_ , host Ayla Brown breaks down the long-running feud between Jimmy Kimmel and President Trump. What started as a late-night appearance years ago has turned into a nearly decade-long back-and-forth involving media giants, political power, and plenty of sharp words. How did it all begin, and where does it stand today? [Listen here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUANDT4C3Wz_8YnIEhUuWlSjt2RBg-CS0xlo2T-vZVe4Ve4Env42IvLuU0Xd_cnU2NxU78XDPXyoutZd5W_p55STx5XufkvzKPLlsEVAfoDkA2uJR3K5tgIjMyNWyOWusesO9kczMnZilVp3yMo4K-U-go9IpYO8Va-SaZXw06oCOAJmwJIBDGOGqv4SRHIYFDqmVpP0ua6o1j3suDl9aXe5PNrdZ5FXQWOAgc9rLVrR1Z4KCPdH7vJ_e6gaChCnrB7tQsJszPlHNalRYSrneX8p9U_9V_GPwJAAD__6dZbGI). You need a gift. We have ideas. Our [2026 Mother's Day Gift Guide](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFywyAQheHTSJ096wUELijSuHPalAwSi7QJEhrAdnz7jNPkAKnfm6_4P67u6jnxNr_7lWyL2W30qIlao-Ja3m-VvncqTNtEdefpK9Hok0PA4QDqAMIFz-npZB9sJEISPdmT1toAGiX7xYIKCKfBGAlmwODPXohRGkHRKzOA6tm-NFAgTgBa4FEABIExjnrScJZjJ4ECt1yOn5m3tlBMz3yncpzy2ie7tLbXTrx1eOnw8jfPHNt840CvX4eXNbeFSj0E_-yLDfuDU-I66E7CvHpOv1z7V4O7xZ8AAAD__3yWcLg) is full of picks that are thoughtful, useful, and won't end up in a closet. Today's sponsor, [Noom](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu4yAUAE-Du1iPBzZQUERa0WXbLS1sHjG7GCybJJvbf-Vf4V9gZjR_btPNp5zK_bffyLZYp0KvM1NrdEyt7o-T_u90JCoLnXta_mWafZ4QcLzAcAExBZ_ye5JdsJEISXRkuVJKA-pBdqsdo5R6VBA8BuBCGrHEaIQkPswAxnTJfmgwgOAASmAvAILAGGe1KDByZhIopFaP_m9Npa0U87s-6eiXunXZrq3tJxNXho6hK7VuvX60uS_UGLqFoZNmAG40Q8e1kQKAodOD4Uy4c_UHhRSY-LX5VK6fjO6wYX-lnNM5KibhvvmUv2XtR4eeFr8CAAD__0bBdQ0), is helping people take control of their weight with a powerful combination of doctor-prescribed treatments and psychology-based coaching--all designed to deliver lasting, sustainable results.

## GOP-led States Consider Map Redraws
The Republican governors of Tennessee and Alabama called state lawmakers into special sessions on Friday, the first steps [to potentially redraw congressional maps](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFyhCAYhJ8GOxwEPbSgSGN3aVM6v_B7kkNh4PfMvX3GvEK6ndmdnd3v6z7dwQe_Pz5hQ0NLnHY8S0AizBPFdBT8SZg97hZL8vYZcIYwSSFvXHRcqMmBD--prZxZECWqCk2jte6F7Lu2Wo1SPQx2Hhq4OWsb1UndNi0M_SJU54SsvLnaRCdUI4RWslZCOCWXZdZWi6GdWSvQeYq5_o5-pxWX8I4vzLWNWxXMSpQKUx9MjkyO53nWdi7Xi8tncrwkkyPhvmMpiBwCzLABJ3giL4SpcIo8o8tw8jUeBfkGqfBypIwbchuPTDwfF6Yr5gtlb8nvDybHKhuXTh-CLzfNWvHYwIe_ZfQvnC8jfwMAAP__ODeM5g) after the Supreme Court narrowed the Voting Rights Act. Alabama lawmakers are set to convene in Montgomery on Monday, as the state awaits a decision from the Supreme Court about whether it can [revert to a previous congressional map](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFygzAQBNCvER2eswQIChVp6Jw2JXOgw1wiJEa62PHfZ8gvpN6Zt7P7cZtuyIHj_R13crKmKdKzBBKhPEk6vgv9HJSZ4kLl4OUr0Ixh0qC7GtoazOSRw2tqKu9WIk2mIne11vag-7apNteTaQbqNa4eZmhXOyB50w3or9h11FbsTg1aMFcAa_TFAHij13W2i4WhmVUD5FlSvnwmjrLRGl7pQfmypL0KbhM5ijJvSo9Kj7LRxiGcmdLjlnY6Byk9FkGh-omybEqPbd-BsV2NAWfcsRaKkUohqjN5LpJ5EY73ulApnOIJVNn548khcOmsauC-I__1VPKv4x5O_wYAAP__OI2Dpg) ahead of the November midterm elections. The Republican-controlled legislature was ordered by the Supreme Court in 2023 to include a second majority-black congressional district. Beginning Tuesday, Tennessee lawmakers could draw a new map that would favor Republicans in all nine House districts, targeting the Memphis-based seat currently held by Democrat Steve Cohen. The [maneuver was made possible](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSpCAURb9GMywElO6AYBOz3nRDC-GhbwfBgmfb_fdTPb8w8a26dc7595gfFiOm9a_dwVDIc4KrRiCCMlM-zgqvAwpCclAPdF8RFhtnwcXI-MC4nL3F-J5V600AECBbML3W-sbFbVDtZsaxd_0YnB2V0n6Ae-Ay3Acp-CLVsqgWzeeND1z2nGspOsm5lyKERTvN72ppFAePlEv3P2OiDUJ85yeUzuW9jWYjOmoj_zRiasR0XVcX8utj8dkbMeUDE-bUiGkHj5blk4pdgdXzKLADc_ksVNkzE6aVFVw3qsw6Yh4cVsyJuRwjeqisgI1I77YYf1wYI9ZRN4qvu8X4Q0O_Svg04jsAAP__LbKJNQ) by the Supreme Court's decision on Wednesday. In a 6-3 ruling, the high court struck down a Louisiana map that includes two majority-black House districts, upholding a lower court ruling that found state lawmakers relied too heavily on race.
## Court Blocks Abortion Pill Access by Mail, Telehealth
A federal appeals court ruled unanimously on Friday to [block the ability of doctors](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHS2yAQRk-DOnnWixD6C4o06pw2pQbBYm28Egwidnz7jJMjpP6-eW_m_bgtN8_Cx_2738m1lJeDXqdQa1SXlsuvk34XqkxHoLNweAitXhYEHHswPeglepb3MnTRJSIk3ZG7WmsnwMkM3eamL-PXaRhNTDRGj1ZHCGDHZFYKtGLH7kMDA_oKYDVeNEDUmNJqg4WvYVUDUOSW6-Vn5qNtlOSdn1QvIe-duK21cir9TeGscG4bbSzy2RTOJQuHt8J5Iy9tC76SwtlMI1y16XdOVCqfLR_UNxL69-pLpTNULo3z0a-Sw4OiwrmrLpYXi_A5WjXAfff8V9S1_yr3dPgnAAD___eGhFA) to prescribe the abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth and mail delivery. The court issued a temporary nationwide injunction that reinstates a requirement that mifepristone must be prescribed and dispensed in person. The Food and Drug Administration permanently lifted the in-person requirement in 2023 after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade in 2022. Telehealth prescriptions have been [key to maintaining abortion access](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-O4yAYxJ_G7rA-g21yBcU16XLtlRZ_PmIunwEBiS9vv_KutC-w5einGc3M39t604FCvP_RO6rm0xrxqIStYVlbys-K_zOWgNFizcE-CI2mlQNfGMwMxOp0oPc69U55RI6iRzVKKS_AL_PUb2p0KEfuEdHBAmiNwGXmi1hGPUvvXB_UmQYziBFACj4IACe490ZaCb8m002ALrRUhn8pxLahp3d6YRls2ntSW2u5duJ3x68dvx7HMURjzxUn7_h1Q01tO1HaMVb2rXXOqKkym56lMUPJPirTJpUWUmQ5EDFtLdbKGhJ-2diuA7FioxaTGKdLX5TLRyAKdZHdBPeTfxZrP3rzpfhHAAAA__8s1Iwf) in states that outlawed or restricted the practice. Surveys have found that the majority of abortions in the U.S. are [provided via pills](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFygzAQRL9GdGIOCZBdqEhD57QpGSFO9sUCMdLZ2H-fIfmENNvszpud93UZL44irddPt6DlkMYV9xKRGfPIaXsUfG2YCVePZSN_jzi5OCpQvYROgh5nR_E9ttVsA6JCXaFtjDEnUKeurW52OjemhdmHrjVdCOi0813fnJ0OTe8MVGQPGnSgGwCjVa0BZq1CmIw3cG4n0QLOxCnX34lWvmGI7_TEXPu0VNHemLci9IdQg1DDvu91SK8G-Hn0Qg0HXagBuiMaoQafHpllxsKZPBfpppSZ0iqd91iKdD6nUuSjyOktp5j8ndarXP48yYUCbpkKpxWFGqps522nGKn0RrRwPXa_z_hfOp9W_QQAAP__yLGKbA) and that about 1 in 4 abortions nationally are prescribed via telehealth.
## List: Top Experiences in the US
A day tour of Lower Antelope Canyon and Horseshoe Bend in Arizona was chosen as [the Top Experience in the U.S.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1ugzAURp8GNtDFP5gMHqq22dIJqSNy7Avc1sGW7ULz9lWz9AG6f_qOznm_TBdDnrblzdxQlzlMGx7ZYymYphLiV8bviIlws5gj2U-PV-MnBqxvQDbAJ2fI3ydROz0jMuQ16k4pNQAbpKhX7SwoKdhpNlKKXjAJfc_FYAbrjO0GqEn_voEE3gEozloO4Dib56uyCk7iWglARyWk9iPQVlac_T3smFobbrXXaykxV_ypYueKnY_jaEuiaNxOOTw2FTuPyezoMeXnNZDFZlxpW_IYXkJjxxBf_xybpTt1ddIuHuQ95V5VApabIf_AlX812jX7CQAA__9yuHxF) in Tripadvisor's 2026 Travelers' Choice Awards. The [Lower Antelope Canyon trip](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M2u0zAQxfGnSXaJ_JWkXWRRQBWL3iuEECxH03jSDLh2ZE8T-vbo9hXYHx399P_1Bm_IgePtHe80ypwg0l4CiVAGSeuj0N-VMlOcqKw8_Ql0xQBGmb5RXaMseOTwBFf7cSYyZGsa9TAMB2UOnauXUXnXm-t86CaNrp_9QKStcVprr7E7HGseP95Up6xWarCmtUp5a-b5OkyDOrpr5RR5lpTb34mjLDSHZ9oot1O612FcRNZS2VNlzpU57_veSuYV_cYlvTaVOZ9EMk7CKX7LyT8m-U4b097cXHfsbeM_xFo73VzSThlOUSikleAzxmeKgNHD15QLlSURfKLo4Qs-4Ud6ZNhZFrg84rQ0Fyzwk25Y4J029Ngucg91Hv26cwhc-qFy6nZHDi-6_FfvbTT_AgAA___xI5iQ) is led by a local Navajo guide who explains the canyon's geology and cultural significance features. **Whatever happened to Tab diet soda?** Tab may be the only soft drink that managed to invent a category, lose it to its own successor, and still inspire petitions for its return decades later. Introduced by Coca-Cola in 1963, Tab was the company's first diet drink, arriving as calorie counting began to enter the American mainstream. Early ads targeted women, tying the drink, with its [iconic pink can](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCnMV3QZm0wFC7S0OWqk65Exl6TvRhjGV9yeftf_K_wlyN9mpHmz32-W46c1l92I1PDPid6H5FqpTLXPf876H-mwpQcHZndM9Ji44yA_QW6C8jZW46fWTXeBCIk2ZC5aq0HwKFTzcNA1wV1VQP1wS4u2EV1yvejptHBuLihYXO2QQfyCqAlthLASwxh0U7DqBahgDzXvbR_d071QSF-9heV1u1bE82j1nwIeRM4CZwotW9-cibPtt3LKnA6s8Dpt11mgYMvnJ4CR4FS4LSdTuA0cSQhb6dxNrU5rU0xPr85Rj56LRSsm-X4PVl_9NPL4FcAAAD__531ezE), to slimness and control. [Here's one of their commercials](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwjAUxfGnsTeiy70OhsFDpYpOsFbqYvnjhpg6cZQYUt6-Sl-h6znSb_h_XuzFpZzG29UNbGpX7MjrkrlWnm0t02Phn4nnxGPgZUrhO7N32SLgYQftDshGl_LLKhlNx4xMks1ea30EPLZK9ubkwJOifauOBAoZOXgfo6KTPkQMrUxm06AF2gNowoYAImHXeR00nJQXCjimWubmXtJYe-7yqzx5bkIZZDZ9rdMi6E3gWeB5XdfmVR714Xn7t8XV0As6PwW980fI169-uFOQs4nTmnJOy0ELBbfBpfxH1n91eBr8DQAA__8VlXEx), featuring the catchy Tab jingle. The strategy worked. By the 1970s, Tab was the dominant diet soda in the United States and helped define an entirely new category. Its demise began with the success of a different kind. In 1982, Coca-Cola launched Diet Coke, a sleeker product backed by the company's flagship brand. Consumers migrated quickly.

Tab, once the innovator, became the legacy option. Health scares around saccharin and recipe tweaks did not help. By the 1990s, Tab had faded from the mainstream, kept alive largely out of habit and loyalty. That loyalty proved unusually durable. Tab developed a devoted following of drinkers who refused to switch, often describing the taste as irreplaceable. When rumors of discontinuation surfaced, fans stockpiled cases and traded tips on where to find remaining cans. Coca-Cola finally ended production in 2020, citing a need to streamline its portfolio. The reaction was immediate. [Petitions circulated, advocacy groups formed, and diehards pushed for a comeback](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAQQL_G7iYaA147BcU16fbaK60xHuy5xYCAJJu_P-V-YesnPT29P5_LJ0mQuP-mk23zaYn8rIFb47K0lO-VvzMX4ei4ZnFfgVcKi0L1ATgC6mUjCa_F9Jv1zIp1z3aYpmlGNY-mPyzjcDXzOGo3z4RMRMqv7rqSIj8rbXqxbxuOqAfESauLRty08n6d3IRXs3YGeZOWyuVvktgO9uGVHlwuLp19sEdruXb6V6dunbplTjnwG3Xq5ilWcHRmkj2CTwUIGq1Q00ZQuN1LBIobtIOlAGdxwN6n0iqc9IKV4ZnKl8Qd-NuFe5UHwzDMWptx6Ivd8lNCkPoxdQb3kyT8T2o_-viw6l8AAAD__0TqiQI). This natural alternative matched results in under two weeks with zero side effects. | Whatever happened to Tab diet soda? Tab may be the only soft drink that managed to invent a category, lose it to its own successor, and still inspire petitions for its return decades later. Introduced by Coca-Cola in 1963, Tab was the company's first diet drink, arriving as calorie counting began to enter the American mainstream. Early ads targeted women, tying the drink, with its , to slimness and control. , featuring the catchy Tab jingle. The strategy worked. By the 1970s, Tab was the dominant diet soda in the United States and helped define an entirely new category. Its demise began with the success of a different kind. In 1982, Coca-Cola launched Diet Coke, a sleeker product backed by the company's flagship brand. Consumers migrated quickly. Tab, once the innovator, became the legacy option. Health scares around saccharin and recipe tweaks did not help. By the 1990s, Tab had faded from the mainstream, kept alive largely out of habit and loyalty. That loyalty proved unusually durable. Tab developed a devoted following of drinkers who refused to switch, often describing the taste as irreplaceable. When rumors of discontinuation surfaced, fans stockpiled cases and traded tips on where to find remaining cans. Coca-Cola finally ended production in 2020, citing a need to streamline its portfolio. The reaction was immediate. . Tab's story is not just about a product that lost market share: It's about what happens when a brand outlives its moment but not its core appeal. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

Can’t find it? That’s because they hid it deep in the fine print.

Most Americans casually clicked "I Agree" to the new terms of service this month, not realizing they just gave permission for the Digital Dollar switch.

This controversial update allows institutions to:

* Track every penny you spend.
* Freeze your account if your purchase history looks "suspicious."
* Cap how much cash you can withdraw.

If you have a checking account or IRA, you are already in the danger zone.

But there is a specific way to reverse this permission before the system goes live.

[Click here to see how to "Uncheck the Box" and shield your wealth.](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=fb227511c59943aba99bc4a2a8c27fbe&_e=tr2X1xMkHFPFc2pRFcFKj3fAJIxDebB1abI3roX6vVFW7KuGFUds_B62rFVi6kzGv8av3IWHd8VfvjluAmjJCBzE67P870XHQq_CyFVvqEXeQrynMlesjT7Ilrt3tF8WKswB0RLYPAj_X3OMZ3avNF7694YL9-9ZAXSDmQDHkgUbF1ZgNXLQyazW_3FBbQyw0-c1QK5gxW5oSK-O0_81sz3zCRXkG9hD7z22RJMQIVBrRUNVY87fnaUqAsBbLffbuIKTTBFo-7zJDG6i4bt86eClAhfebmTqK4rVnI2ZRinWyFKp0h1GFT_DCc7EkBSDI_XdbQhJ8WLTT_ES1-IPIvJyFTaFjUNAjOsnOglDDlv-zIEdJGj12oYj4k-v5f03Tv6REhDJh_QMLoAINK5_JH9alBay9aLQ7Q7FLVCDIn2P8B54DFbll33Dl0YH4C9w07ZHO1q3EBw8a4A6i4n8IUw9qL7I9A2Ot81oi8eAEsRD2QmdYAXFM4DZUG1ON8iU8yZxwtQ6dzepFIHAMhwZ-IGb4YtXqn2hJuLqSMobT1xkIgRGhia5bxYUpL8RWhhtwcCVcsnAm_uxVXVx1Uy5gBESVk-AZld6Lfd1Ubx3ts6yzTx31E5cS0watUWg-4s4)

And look for the new "Digital Integration" tab. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | Open your banking app. Go to settings. And look for the new "Digital Integration" tab. | Can’t find it? That’s because they hid it deep in the fine print. Most Americans casually clicked "I Agree" to the new terms of service this month, not realizing they just gave permission for the Digital Dollar switch. This controversial update allows institutions to: Track every penny you spend. Freeze your account if your purchase history looks "suspicious." Cap how much cash you can withdraw. If you have a checking account or IRA, you are already in the danger zone. But there is a specific way to reverse this permission before the system goes live. ## Gas Prices Pummel Texas Wallets
Gas [prices surged nationwide](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDtygzAURVcDnRj9-BUq0tA5bUrmIT2JN5GBkRRj7z6Ds4R0t7ifOffrNt-AIm3hE-5oytPv84ZnjlgKpjlAPhJZzPknBVwRUomwOR9nyWXHeMu4mh1QfM26dmbhbhQ1GtH3_cA7Idt6NWCHbrTOjVZ61KNaNHbtOAzotcPF9jWZq4y3XAnBJRfNwnFovUKpRutFryrN0VHZU1NWLPiE7ONrf2Bq7H6vo1lLOXKlPio5VXI6z7Px-1NfGJehktMlKzm9oyxAZn9QLFFGtkGhfYPI4IEJArJ80DdmBgFoq5Nxx0kxUu76SvNwB4rv2fK_sx5G_gYAAP__nrOBUg) to $4.39, the highest in four years, while Texas averages climbed to $3.86 as oil topped $100 per barrel, AAA reported Friday. Dallas-Fort Worth drivers are averaging around $3.99 per gallon, while El Paso has hit $4.20. Conflict with Iran [has pushed global oil prices](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uxCAQhJ8Gd5yW5Xy2C4o07i5tSrSGxUbibAvI_bx95IvyBCl3ZzSjb76u9koxxXX-pBub-gybXflREtfK2c5U9hwdl_KdZ16Yck20-pAsAl4ktBK09RTTy54bbybwg2rYqK7rergobJvFkNMtgevVQBMMnoPS2OvAjA7VAEMTzREGLWilAEGdJuC-DZpRDy6oToszsI91y6e6cOUnlZBe253zyW23Jpml1r0I_SFwFDi6qRwEhyZwfNsFjsfr75QzFfnLJXMsLGOmVbptDSm62mTj90dMKZZLJ84w3yimd1P93z53gz8BAAD__zooe2A) higher, driving gas prices up across Texas and nationwide, with experts warning costs could keep climbing as Middle East tensions disrupt supply.
## Texas Picks Mayors, Senate Seat
Texas voters headed to the polls Saturday to decide mayoral races in four of the state's 100 largest cities and a state Senate special election, with [returns coming in](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzz2O6yAUQOHVmC7W5c-EguI16fLaKdG1udhIxLaACcnuR5ktTH2kTzpfd3_HlNO-_scHufaKh9-p10ytUfEr1rOkhWr9LitthKVl3EPMXoCYLqAvIH3AlN9eseBmCJYzctwYc4WJC802B6i5EtoIzS3NQaFVYOdlApjjZMGy5D4YaJCcgwA-zkBXHSUJaZfIjRwUUEjtKGPbqNELa8zv40llXI4Hy25r7ayD_DeI2yBuvfexR8RPHMSNMi0tHXtlxYWzp5xTncygYH1gyr9C-9v304mfAAAA__9oXmwU) overnight and runoffs possible in several contests. [Arlington's nonpartisan race](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAMx58GuoyMQwgpKK6h22uvRJ7EDJE8wyjxLsvbn9jq-ut-kuX_15-P5YOy5NfjNz150u91X158VGFVLsuD6rvkyLV-lgdvTEWFXmmVBQEHA86AXRJlOZe-TdMdUuhanjrv_QhDh67dJgg0prsP6EIkXgND9D0ScAAHKVCbp0sMHNiuA4Tudgce3WoZbYhr523TA6ese7npxsrfVFc59y8ut7g_W5k21Xdt7K8G5wbn4zhux0p0HRucqWiOwg3OV68G5_cuWXO8kIWj5v31L5tEp_kxMYlEqJp1L2qOvehmnnQaNIXrp-j1hKM3iGzvw9CZ6IM3vRvJjDE5M0TnnO3tQERtmdL7yCK5Dr7p4fGkLD_p9f82_5rwbwAAAP__mkiU6g) drew the most attention. Incumbent Mayor Jim Ross, a 65-year-old attorney, former Marine, and onetime Arlington police officer, faced real estate developer Steve Cavender along with challengers Hunter Crow and Shaun Mallory. Ross is seeking his final allowed term under Arlington's three-term cap. Mayoral races in Lubbock, Irving, and Frisco were also on the ballot, with term-limited incumbents stepping aside in Irving and Frisco. Any runoffs go to June 13. In Senate District 4, Republican Brett Ligon, former Montgomery County district attorney, faced Democrat Ron Angeletti to fill the seat vacated by Brandon Creighton, who resigned last fall to lead the Texas Tech University System. The deep-red district stretches from Houston's northern suburbs to the Louisiana coast--Creighton won it by roughly 70% to 30% in 2022. The winner serves through January, when the same pair meets again in November. [See the breakdown](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDHSozAMhU9jOjOybExSuNiG7t92S8YBGTxjMGMrIbn9DtkjbCM133zSe39-xh8fU9yX334jx--Qx53OmoiZyrj4epQ4Ua3PstBKvnDy-xzSiIBWQidBj7OP6TOaZnYPmO-qIaf6vr-BVdg1q9P-YT2BNR6tRTQBDBkI6m51CMH2TXSXDDrQSgGCah9Aty5oQn2fguq1MEBz5FxaXonp7WtIn_yi0k55a5JbmY8q9C-Bg8DhPM8We-B8xv0CBA5XIoHDdUbgAN01UODwdclXZqoy5GeRU-RIVVKiieXmP7nIyp5JVtq_66Ap-vQPiHkf1Q1Mu_KWmuLm44wpxWp7YWDZfEzf__j_Wn05_BsAAP__AiWL3w).
## World Cup Trophy Makes Houston Stop
The [FIFA World Cup Trophy](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ8GOtB6DXZSuLgmXa69Ehl7wZYcjOwlJG9_4qrr_3a-0Yz0_XlOTxtT3Nbf9kWGP0ueNjprImYq02rrXqKjWo-yUiBbONnNL2lCQNXB2IGcvI3pOw2tNzP4u2jJCK31DZTAsQ1mvHkBDt2I2hMqpZSevZR3tDCjmnUbzTUGI0ghAEH0M9BtXCShvLtFaNkMQD5yLj0HYvrYuqRvflPpXX61yQTmvTbyV4OPBh_nefYhH5Xz5kLJW3SJruKFckm-c8fe4MMWvsj_accl7-HbcT5Kh4gS1DD2e9jbYvx-xpRiVboZYH3ZmP6d88-UvQ3-DQAA___-HoEF) stops in Houston today, offering fans a final chance to see it in Texas before the tour moves on. Houston [will host seven](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAUhJ_GdKx8AbwULtLQ_WlTooN9vFjyBdmHZfftI_IKqaaY0ejT9-dn_YEQQ379hoSGPr6sGa8WkQjr-oJ21GCxtbO-cEeoFCE7H1fJ5dTzsedqdRDidx06ZzbuZtGhEVrrJ5-EHLvdDMiVHt3s7czdNExuU3IW26zGzevnoLtg7jM-ciUEl1w8No7P0SuUarZeaMUGji5QqQ_akfADzcdveWN92JK6aHaiozH1i8mFyeW6rocPHu6SyQUzkwuVs2ZImKkxuSTMd1ylRmfPg8nFQgYHCT_BlrPBzcPkApWCjXhv93I2KrlvBC6cqd9Lo77hG3OfgOyOravGHVeIMbRJs4G_EoT4D5D-T-vbyL8BAAD__y1Ajrk) 2026 FIFA World Cup matches at NRG Stadium, including five group games and two knockout rounds, featuring teams like Germany and Portugal. In fact, most valuations are based on "mass appraisal" models that don't account for the specifics of your property. [That's where Ownwell comes in](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAUhZ8Gb4kuYBtnYKhUeUvXjugCFxsJ_whInLx9lahSH6Dr-c75hvN9NVeMKa7TFy6k6yNsZqWjJKqVspmw7Dk6KuWWJ5oJc024-pCMANGfoDuBNB5jepq28dqCv_CGNFdKDdBz0TWzHiwP0qO9eBfsAE4FP7RoA-9tUFwMTdQvGXQgOQcB_GyBhi5IEvLiAleStUA-1i2f60yVHlhCem53yme3LU3Sc607kx9MjEyM27EelNILMTG-20yOt7qYst2yIyY_f9dM9K_Y4bJjnNY_YBZ8ir7J2u9HTCmWXrEWpgXjW9vU__101-InAAD__wG-fxo). They handle the entire protest process for you, reviewing your valuation, filing your protest, and negotiating directly with the appraisal district. There's no upfront cost, and you only pay if they save you money. The results speak for themselves. Homeowners who protest see meaningful reductions, [and a large percentage of cases result in savings](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAUhZ8Gb4kuYBtnYKhUeUvXjugCFxsJ_whInLx9lahSH6Dr-c75hvN9NVeMKa7TFy6k6yNsZqWjJKqVspmw7Dk6KuWWJ5oJc024-pCMANGfoDuBNB5jepq28dqCv_CGNFdKDdBz0TWzHiwP0qO9eBfsAE4FP7RoA-9tUFwMTdQvGXQgOQcB_GyBhi5IEvLiAleStUA-1i2f60yVHlhCem53yme3LU3Sc607kx9MjEyM27EelNILMTG-20yOt7qYst2yIyY_f9dM9K_Y4bJjnNY_YBZ8ir7J2u9HTCmWXrEWpgXjW9vU__101-InAAD__wG-fxo). Guy, our founder, has personally used Ownwell and saved thousands. [With the May 15 deadline approaching fast](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAUhZ8Gb4kuYBtnYKhUeUvXjugCFxsJ_whInLx9lahSH6Dr-c75hvN9NVeMKa7TFy6k6yNsZqWjJKqVspmw7Dk6KuWWJ5oJc024-pCMANGfoDuBNB5jepq28dqCv_CGNFdKDdBz0TWzHiwP0qO9eBfsAE4FP7RoA-9tUFwMTdQvGXQgOQcB_GyBhi5IEvLiAleStUA-1i2f60yVHlhCem53yme3LU3Sc607kx9MjEyM27EelNILMTG-20yOt7qYst2yIyY_f9dM9K_Y4bJjnNY_YBZ8ir7J2u9HTCmWXrEWpgXjW9vU__101-InAAD__wG-fxo), now is the time to act. clean_body: Texas gas statewide average, up from $2.55 in February, marking one of the steepest short-term spikes in years. | | | | | | Sunday, May 3, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1903, daredevil of Corpus Christi performed his loop-the-loop stunt on a bicycle before King Edward VII in London. Check today's Et Cetera section to will mark Cinco de Mayo with festivals, food, and live entertainment across the region, with events from Carthage to Tyler featuring music, dancing, vendors, and family-friendly fun. Abilene ☁️ 79/55° | Amarillo ☁️ 82/46° | Austin ☁️ 76/52° | Brownsville ☁️ 78/61° | Corpus Christi ☁️ 78/60° | DFW ☁️ 84/57° | El Paso ☁️ 84/53° | Houston 🌫️️ 75/53° | Laredo ☁️ 83/58° | Lubbock ☁️ 78/49° | Midland ☁️ 80/52° | San Antonio ☁️ 76/54° | Tyler 🌥️ 80/53° | Waco ☁️ 76/54° Take The Flyover with you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Gas Prices Pummel Texas Wallets Gas to $4.39, the highest in four years, while Texas averages climbed to $3.86 as oil topped $100 per barrel, AAA reported Friday. Dallas-Fort Worth drivers are averaging around $3.99 per gallon, while El Paso has hit $4.20. Conflict with Iran higher, driving gas prices up across Texas and nationwide, with experts warning costs could keep climbing as Middle East tensions disrupt supply. Texas Picks Mayors, Senate Seat Texas voters headed to the polls Saturday to decide mayoral races in four of the state's 100 largest cities and a state Senate special election, with overnight and runoffs possible in several contests. drew the most attention. Incumbent Mayor Jim Ross, a 65-year-old attorney, former Marine, and onetime Arlington police officer, faced real estate developer Steve Cavender along with challengers Hunter Crow and Shaun Mallory. Ross is seeking his final allowed term under Arlington's three-term cap. Mayoral races in Lubbock, Irving, and Frisco were also on the ballot, with term-limited incumbents stepping aside in Irving and Frisco. Any runoffs go to June 13. In Senate District 4, Republican Brett Ligon, former Montgomery County district attorney, faced Democrat Ron Angeletti to fill the seat vacated by Brandon Creighton, who resigned last fall to lead the Texas Tech University System. The deep-red district stretches from Houston's northern suburbs to the Louisiana coast--Creighton won it by roughly 70% to 30% in 2022. The winner serves through January, when the same pair meets again in November. . World Cup Trophy Makes Houston Stop The stops in Houston today, offering fans a final chance to see it in Texas before the tour moves on. The trophy arrived on Friday as part of its North American tour, with today being the final public display day. It's before the 2026 World Cup kicks off June 11. Houston 2026 FIFA World Cup matches at NRG Stadium, including five group games and two knockout rounds, featuring teams like Germany and Portugal. Fans lined up this weekend for a rare close-up with the iconic gold prize, that will be lifted by the tournament's champion. | Flying together with our sponsor Every year, Texas homeowners receive an appraised value for their home, but that number isn't always accurate. In fact, most valuations are based on "mass appraisal" models that don't account for the specifics of your property. . They handle the entire protest process for you, reviewing your valuation, filing your protest, and negotiating directly with the appraisal district. There's no upfront cost, and you only pay if they save you money. The results speak for themselves. Homeowners who protest see meaningful reductions, . Guy, our founder, has personally used Ownwell and saved thousands. , now is the time to act. Check your home and see if you can save. | ➤ Houston: Homebuyers are flocking to smaller, more affordable Houston-area communities like Huffman, where new construction and lower prices are driving a surge in sales outside traditional hot spots. () ➤ Denton: Texas Woman's University kinesiology students won a NASA-backed design competition with a pneumatic boot that stimulates foot sensors to help prevent muscle loss during long-duration spaceflight. () ➤ Southlake: police credited Detective Jamie Hannah with unraveling a $300,000 money laundering scheme, leading to a 2025 arrest after years of financial analysis and coordination with multiple banks. () ➤ San Antonio: Federal prosecutors in the Western District of Texas filed 253 new immigration-related criminal cases last week as enforcement activity continued at an elevated pace. () ➤ Fort Worth: The Heartland Flyer will shut down within 90 days after Texas and Oklahoma lawmakers declined funding, ending the 206-mile Amtrak route linking Fort Worth and Oklahoma City after more than two decades. () ➤ Garland: Goodwill reopened its expanded 31,000-square-foot store on Thursday, making it Texas' largest location and boosting inventory with about 10,000 new items added daily. subject: BREAKING: Republicans TAKE THE LEAD
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
################################# | Peter,

Our party’s position is getting stronger and stronger ahead of the midterms.

Polling shows Republicans have an **8-POINT LEAD** over radical far-Left Democrats.

On top of that, Senate Republicans **OUTRAISED** Senate Democrats by $8 MILLION in 2025!

Given the strong financial position our party is in, we’re not going to ask you for money.

** Instead, we’re laser-focused on securing our lead by focusing our time and resources on the ONE THING that actually wins elections: _[Votes!](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=599098191fd343a7bf3c896b39f3fe30&_e=PCS0blhT3gzFl8OeEz7F0yxxv0-8GI-7A8pLz98ILpfU6Rp3VeZ2vYNuUj2gmC5L8F702lDQu-ToTa8xB6lRwZHTGTY4ESqzEk30BeZCxF8HflO_z0X5cOKndTW6dhVtR2vUrye7CHUfDtNyteDkIZ4bgPJlYSmCUnDN1hVEXJzYPAEbZryAJfdfUNuZoM8joMovSYIsMI74Eu_bDEfXyaTA0nR85SopwuHTDkqHfxxVXBSPt-ayCs7uwIr0NBJKJXZFnl5of-5kUe2K-dt8kR8GK53nHQxmu_52Oo9LH99DRiAKpqvlI6XBFZJazBfxaMD-dYu6lSPt45G-Im6XyWrW7OlyP7ChiIlmgxcI65_yS_2V6Dl4lkNC9wl-dtmdXG88x8hl2ievVz1A8f0J0rWQsdR2P68bNWdn5HLzkDOK4D6vAVgxMcjBhX56Ujq2kTTbwPjD-v4gQLdfcmsb48FAxSvvheDRqkl3sC_noW-KMPsNuHVtkBB6100Cv0EK3uuT6hNkKUvT1Z1ARkbWIdgE3_oLPpo-Wb6oi_pYbIoZ7ADD_bQdeRkikx-ILZ222L5UGb7j7IdyGpMuQzpYzeRM1iVn1YnD0ipUCx4Dq_ztjnT1t0U4bcQ86kuehORgDOqviWq_akgGKnvYIlLCXOFU7aEOc60ANnwHY_wyMmWEo9kfICQqyCk60P1ZtVtSr2fKSc3VQ4bRxdJIkw4pnj0P4lSEc3O9l81udHOwCqDMNin764i4UUdIQPuzwoHa9NX7O4ybLLe_N_WOGJTvnFllriH2JdhE7cJU71D8do1KIqDELe9Wv6xQzEb8ca5Uabelz_L-Da6LCtLZlXK6Uh-hxlJMLZtu3rEMthuJ_iQ%3D)_ **

_We can have the best polls and the highest enthusiasm, but without a complete and verified voter profile from every Republican, we’ll still be in danger of LOSING to the Democrats._

Instead, we’re laser-focused on securing our lead by focusing our time and resources on the ONE THING that actually wins elections: We can have the best polls and the highest enthusiasm, but without a complete and verified voter profile from every Republican, we’ll still be in danger of LOSING to the Democrats. name: Defend Our Courts
subject: Wisconsin Public Radio covered our secret weapon
body:
| | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below.
---
| [Unsubscribe](https://audience.constantcontact.com/preferences/unsubscribe?m=001TiGr7G8G0an2bTkrhnK17w==&c=372e9de6-c0cc-11f0-8fbb-0242a0b0d8a1&ca=571cffa7-9f88-47cd-a7a3-1105e522c9bf) | [Update Profile](https://audience.constantcontact.com/preferences/update/init?ch=374622f4-c0cc-11f0-8fbb-0242a0b0d8a1&c=372e9de6-c0cc-11f0-8fbb-0242a0b0d8a1&t=001wShnsoVNDOjiJ698orYWxQ==&m=001TiGr7G8G0an2bTkrhnK17w==&ca=571cffa7-9f88-47cd-a7a3-1105e522c9bf) | [Constant Contact Data Notice](https://www.constantcontact.com/legal/customer-contact-data-notice)
---
---
| | [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wOvnRAZBWOxu3-RZBZSVwfu1yXgKhIojO2joAgUCV4pWBhEgrKuOw6WhFY3IUWQhnI9b-enPE1x2YIk8Dwy2-Zr8fYfVlsHrIeVfrCqCtksPA-fQj6i20MykrQxuvLDWiBfP4imWLD8CGXLiL4xJQtx8OCabH-15fbcPJDJaV5kUYt9gfsEp5Fm7wB3gl0iPxlZT3OPd-zbbX3raZZ9d_hiUuH7TzoDQU_pSWsd3M-qI-e_yfRkO31Gi5qfIQiOe&c=C8cM4K_1rOuQlv8oyXKmtce67WvF5IYA7V0sjw1nBs0OwKiFbvLzmA==&ch=vuM7mFisMpXg8vbFlW36n3cww4YcJeFb3jCap9T9H0n81dalgYkSqw==)
---
| Friend,
We did something different in Wisconsin, and people are sitting up and taking notice. With a smaller budget than our Pennsylvania Supreme Court race in 2025, we couldn't run a full field and TV campaign. Traditional organizers. Mailers. Ads on every screen.
That wasn't in the cards.
So instead of doing less of what everyone else does, we did something no one else was doing.
Our co-founder Joe Bachman was just interviewed by Wisconsin Public Radio about the cutting-edge voter contact technology we deployed in the state and how it helped us punch way above our weight:
---
| [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wOvnRAZBWOxu3-RZBZSVwfu1yXgKhIojO2joAgUCV4pWBhEgrKuOw6WhFY3IUWQhnI9b-enPE1x2YIk8Dwy2-Zr8fYfVlsHrIeVfrCqCtksPA-fQj6i20MykrQxuvLDWiBfP4imWLD8CGXLiL4xJQtx8OCabH-15fbcPJDJaV5kUYt9gfsEp5Fm7wB3gl0iPxlZT3OPd-zbbX3raZZ9d_hiUuH7TzoDQU_pSWsd3M-qI-e_yfRkO3zltSFs2Au_H&c=C8cM4K_1rOuQlv8oyXKmtce67WvF5IYA7V0sjw1nBs0OwKiFbvLzmA==&ch=vuM7mFisMpXg8vbFlW36n3cww4YcJeFb3jCap9T9H0n81dalgYkSqw==)
---
| Here's the core idea: every Democrat deserves an organizer in their pocket.
An actual, interactive tool built specifically to talk a voter through exactly what they needed to know. Where to vote, what ID to bring, how the process works. One that could speak to Chris Taylor's record and positions on the issues. One that could answer questions with accurate information, at any hour, at a massive scale: we used it to reach over 1 million voters.
We share the concerns about AI's energy demands, economic disruption, and impact on workers. That's why we worked with our partners at Convos to keep our footprint minimal. And for what it's worth: this approach has a smaller environmental impact than a large mailer campaign.
AI is becoming a bigger part of political campaigns. As we read stories about deepfakes and misinformation, Defend Our Courts is proud to lead the way in using this technology responsibly and transparently to have real conversations with the voters we need to reach. This also isn't about replacing the field organizers and volunteers who are the backbone of our democracy. It's about giving them a force multiplier. Reaching voters they couldn't reach. Having conversations that would otherwise never happen.
Wisconsin showed it can work and we couldn't have done it without you.
Tens of thousands of Democrats across the country pitched in to fuel this work. You made a difference in a race that far too few people were paying attention to.
Court races are like that. Under the radar. Underestimated. Until suddenly they're not. 2026 will have more high-stakes judicial races, with the same type of stakes as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania. We want to continue bringing our winning formula to key races on a national scale. But we need your continued support to do it:
[If you believe in what we're building and what we proved is possible, please consider chipping in $25, $15, or whatever you can spare today to help us build for more court battles in 2026?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wOvnRAZBWOxu3-RZBZSVwfu1yXgKhIojO2joAgUCV4pWBhEgrKuOw6WhFY3IUWQhnI9b-enPE1x2YIk8Dwy2-Zr8fYfVlsHrIeVfrCqCtksPA-fQj6i20MykrQxuvLDWiBfP4imWLD8CGXLiL4xJQtx8OCabH-15fbcPJDJaV5kUYt9gfsEp5Fm7wB3gl0iPxlZT3OPd-zbbX3raZZ9d_hiUuH7TzoDQU_pSWsd3M-qI-e_yfRkO3ydvZar2FF3p&c=C8cM4K_1rOuQlv8oyXKmtce67WvF5IYA7V0sjw1nBs0OwKiFbvLzmA==&ch=vuM7mFisMpXg8vbFlW36n3cww4YcJeFb3jCap9T9H0n81dalgYkSqw==)

clean_body: | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | | == | Friend, We did something different in Wisconsin, and people are sitting up and taking notice. With a smaller budget than our Pennsylvania Supreme Court race in 2025, we couldn't run a full field and TV campaign. Traditional organizers. Mailers. Ads on every screen. That wasn't in the cards. So instead of doing less of what everyone else does, we did something no one else was doing. Our co-founder Joe Bachman was just interviewed by Wisconsin Public Radio about the cutting-edge voter contact technology we deployed in the state and how it helped us punch way above our weight: | == | Here's the core idea: every Democrat deserves an organizer in their pocket. An actual, interactive tool built specifically to talk a voter through exactly what they needed to know. Where to vote, what ID to bring, how the process works. One that could speak to Chris Taylor's record and positions on the issues. One that could answer questions with accurate information, at any hour, at a massive scale: we used it to reach over 1 million voters. We share the concerns about AI's energy demands, economic disruption, and impact on workers. That's why we worked with our partners at Convos to keep our footprint minimal. And for what it's worth: this approach has a smaller environmental impact than a large mailer campaign. AI is becoming a bigger part of political campaigns. As we read stories about deepfakes and misinformation, Defend Our Courts is proud to lead the way in using this technology responsibly and transparently to have real conversations with the voters we need to reach. This also isn't about replacing the field organizers and volunteers who are the backbone of our democracy. It's about giving them a force multiplier. Reaching voters they couldn't reach. Having conversations that would otherwise never happen. Wisconsin showed it can work and we couldn't have done it without you. Tens of thousands of Democrats across the country pitched in to fuel this work. You made a difference in a race that far too few people were paying attention to. Court races are like that. Under the radar. Underestimated. Until suddenly they're not. 2026 will have more high-stakes judicial races, with the same type of stakes as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania. We want to continue bringing our winning formula to key races on a national scale. But we need your continued support to do it: == | | | | | == | == | == | == Thank you for being in this with us. Team Defend our Courts | | == | Defend Our Courts | P.O. Box 45569 | Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | |

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: The Truth is coming out, without signatures
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
This is why the President and I did NOT take plea deals.

This is why the President and I did NOT take plea deals.
| **_FROM THE DESK OF RUDY W. GIULIANI_**
---
Dear Friend,
---
When I was arrested in Fulton County in the most violent jail in America, I could’ve made all the legal battles magically disappear overnight...
---
_...I could’ve betrayed the truth by going on camera, dropping to my knees, and saying that the 2020 election was perfectly fair, transparent, and completely free of fraud!_
---
In fact, some who were arrested __did__ take plea deals.
---
**But the President and I did NOT.**
---
Our position was clear: **__NEVER_ _SURRENDER_!_**
---
As the old saying goes, “I’d rather die on my feet than live on my knees.”
---
_And now, the TRUTH is finally coming out:_
---
**“Fulton County admits to verifying 315,000 votes in 2020 without poll worker signatures”**
_-Atlanta News First_
---
But unfortunately, bloodthirsty left-wing tyrants do not care about the truth.
---
**I am _still_ being PROSECUTED by a vengeful Democrat Attorney General who wants to imprison me as part of her re-election campaign platform _this_ year.**
---
And with reports of a blue wave coming, she thinks she can get away with it – because voters will not stop her.
---
_Well, I beg to differ..._
---
_The people can stop her right here, right now by signing onto the People’s Defense of Rudy W. Giuliani in light of new evidence that adds even MORE doubt to the integrity of the 2020 election._

Yes! I’m really asking for just one single dollar. Allow me to explain why just one dollar makes a difference:
Your $1 helps me keep fighting for the wins—like fighting Pentagon price gouging, PFAS cleanup, supporting more competition in military contracting, boosting our industrial base, and strengthening the "right to repair" for our troops.
Your $1 helps me keep fighting for even _more_ good to be done—like urging Congress to pass my Railway Safety Act and my No Corporate Crooks Act.
Your $1 helps me fight for transparency in our government—from banning congressional stock trading to calling for the Epstein files to be released.
And your $1 helps me stand up to Donald Trump and his pathetic agenda—whether it’s defending democracy, pushing back against Trump’s effort to strip federal workers of protections, or rolling back health care for millions of Americans.
Look, I know there’s a lot of bad stuff coming out of Washington these days. And I know it can be frustrating—believe me, I feel it too.
But that’s exactly the moment we stiffen our spines and stand our ground. We can’t let Donald Trump and his kiss-ass Congressional allies win again—not in Washington, and definitely not here in Western Pennsylvania.
If we want to take back the House in 2026 and stop Trump’s weak agenda, then we have to hold the line here in Pennsylvania. It’s the only Democratic-held battleground seat in the state—and the GOP knows flipping this district puts them one step closer to a majority.
[**That’s why I’m asking for just $1. So Derek, can I count on you to pitch in?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130643296/618462042/1285128406?amounts=1,15,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=email_050326_RSone&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMzEwODk5ZC1lNzQ2LWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nLMLVZzy-x1Bf_5RHrRBfRXp0_tzV-BlSQ0rQ84xpA0=)
| [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130643299/618462045/1285128406?amounts=1,15,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=email_050326_RSone&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMzEwODk5ZC1lNzQ2LWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nLMLVZzy-x1Bf_5RHrRBfRXp0_tzV-BlSQ0rQ84xpA0=)
